c74object.name: buildspans
c74object.digest: Assembles, validates, and manages multi-bar spans of notes and offsets for music transcripts
c74object.description:
The buildspans object is a sophisticated, thread-safe manager for musical event transcripts. It aggregates individual note-level and offset-level performance events and synthesizes them into coherent, multi-bar spans on a per-track, per-palette basis.

Core Architectural Mechanisms

1. Offset Resolution Hierarchy

To correctly assign note events to their logical bars, the object resolves the temporal offset of incoming notes using a strict two-tier lookup hierarchy:
  • Tier 1 (Active Span Dictionary Lookup): The object checks the existing active span database in x->building for the specific palette and track-offset grouping. If a high-precision offset has already been established by a prior note in this span, it is retrieved and used as the authoritative offset.
  • Tier 2 (Effective Global Offset): If no active span is found, the object falls back to the current effective global offset (set via Inlet 2 or via auto-initialization).

2. Ephemeral Auto-Initialization

If no manual global offset is currently set (i.e., current_offset <= 0.0), the object enters an Ephemeral Auto-Initialization mode. In this mode, the absolute synthesized timestamp of the first received note event is dynamically used as the temporary offset for that processing cycle without persistently overwriting the global state.

3. Deferred Rating-Based Span Ending

Spans are evaluated dynamically on note boundaries. A deferred rating check occurs when a note triggers a new bar. The object calculates a Rating Score for the active span (defined as the lowest mean note score of any single bar in the span multiplied by the total number of bars). The object will retroactively end and prune the active span *before* the newest bar if:
  • Including the newest bar would decrease the overall multi-bar rating score.
  • The newest bar's own average score (mean) is higher than the consolidated span rating score if it were included (indicating it is statistically better off starting a new, standalone span).
Once calculated, this span rating is back-propagated and cached across all bars in the span.

4. Discontiguity Assessment

The object performs an event-driven discontiguity assessment when a new bar is detected. A gap limit threshold is calculated as:
limit = most_recent_bar_timestamp + 2.0 * bar_length
If the relative timestamp of the incoming note exceeds this limit, the object identifies a logical gap (mathematically equivalent to at least one full bar of absolute silence), closes/outputs the active span, and starts a new one.

5. Reset Behavior of Bang vs. Flush

  • Bang: A bang triggers a total system flush across all palettes. To prepare the object for a completely new session, it resets the cached local bar length to zero, forcing a re-read from the bound buffer~ upon the next note event.
  • Flush: A flush or flush <track> message ends and outputs the active spans for specific tracks but preserves the cached local bar length state.

inlet.digest: Inlet 1: Note Events, Bang (Flush), or Clear
inlet.description:
Accepts note performance events or control messages:

Note Event Lists:
  • [timestamp, score]: Processes absolute timestamp and score.
  • [calc_timestamp, score, store_timestamp]: The first float (calc_timestamp) is used to compute the logical bar boundary, while the third float (store_timestamp) is preserved inside the dictionary database.
Control Messages:
  • bang: Flushes all currently active spans across all tracks and palettes, and resets local_bar_length to 0.
  • clear: Resets working memory dictionaries, tracking states, global offsets, and cached bar lengths to defaults.
  • flush <track-number>: Ends and outputs all spans for the specified track number across all palettes while preserving local_bar_length.
  • set_bar_buffer <buffer-name>: Binds to a buffer~ to dynamically read bar length (defaults to "bar").

inlet.digest: Inlet 2: Global Offset and Loop Start
inlet.description:
Receives the global alignment parameters for incoming events.

  • float: Sets the high-precision global offset timestamp in milliseconds. Updating this value to a different rounded integer triggers automated span duplication for all active tracks to maintain continuity across sections.
  • list [offset, loop_start]: Sets both the high-precision global offset and the loop start correction. The loop start is added during logical absolute bar timestamp calculations.
Sets the active track context (integer) for subsequently received note events.
Sets the active palette context (symbol) for subsequently received note events.

Sets an explicit local bar length in milliseconds. If this value is greater than 0, it overrides any values in the referenced buffer~. Setting this to 0 or <= 0 tells the object to fall back to the buffer and dynamically read the bar length.

outlet.description: Outputs a list of integers representing the logical bar timestamps comprising the completed span. Bypassed if the @bind attribute is active.
Outputs the track number integer associated with the completed span. Bypassed if the @bind attribute is active.

When a span completes, this outlet outputs key-value data for every bar in the span with prefixed routing selectors in the format: track_num::bar_timestamp::property (properties include absolutes, scores, mean, offset, palette, rating, and span). Bypassed if the @bind attribute is active.
Outputs status and diagnostic log strings, and provides real-time JSON memory states if the @visualize attribute is active.

method.name: list
method.description: Expects either [timestamp, score] or [calc_timestamp, score, store_timestamp].

method.name: bang
method.digest: Force-flushes all spans and resets cached bar length
method.description: Ends and outputs all open spans across all tracks/palettes, resetting local_bar_length to 0.

method.name: flush
method.description: Ends and outputs all open spans for the specified track across all palettes while preserving the cached bar length.

method.name: clear
method.description: Deletes all active spans, resets global offsets, clearing all dictionaries completely.

method.name: set_bar_buffer
method.digest: Specify the bar length buffer source
method.description: Sets the name of the external buffer~ containing the bar length (ms) in its first sample frame.

attribute.name: bind
attribute.description: Binds directly to a named crucible object in the patcher environment. When bound, buildspans bypasses its standard outlet output system (Outlets 1, 2, and 3) and directly calls C API functions in the bound crucible instance for ultra-low latency, zero-copy coordination.

attribute.name: log
attribute.description: When active (1), outputs verbose mathematical decision details, rating evaluation comparisons, and gap checking limits. Additionally, successfully completed or pruned spans containing three or more bars with a negative rating (< 0.0) automatically generate a detailed report file under a logs/ directory situated adjacent to the compiled external object binary itself. &description>

attribute.name: visualize
attribute.description: When active (1), outputs real-time, comprehensive JSON snapshots of the internal hierarchical database structure to the rightmost outlet, enabling interactive visual monitoring.

attribute.name: defer
attribute.description: When active (1), all state-altering operations, dictionary lookups, and span constructions are deferred to the Main (UI) thread. This prevents high-priority scheduler/audio thread locking and protects audio stream stability.

attribute.name: async
attribute.description: When active (1), all dictionary allocations, traversals, rating assessments, and outputs are executed on a dedicated, background worker thread. If the object is also bound via @bind to an async crucible, both objects seamlessly share the same worker thread to preserve strict chronological operation order without thread context switching overhead.
